question: In May two thousand and one, Liu Xiang won his first international one hundred and ten meter hurdles race in Japan. In two thousand and four, he won the first Olympic gold medal for China in the one hundred and ten meter hurdles race, and at the same time broke the Olympic Games record. It was also the first time an Olympic gold medal for hurdling was hung round the neck of a sportsperson from an Asian country. Liu Xiang trained very hard.
